body_txt: Lots of people like names with a meaningful but simple pun , like naming a normal guy "Norm", or naming a not-so-normal guy "Norm" , or naming a jerk "Dick" . But you're not exactly naming a normal guy here, because one way or another, death follows him like a shadow, so you've got to name him in such a way, but you don't want to go for something too fancy or too scary , you've gotta be subtle. Well, subtle in certain languages anyway, as "mort" means "death" in languages like French, stemming from the Latin "mors", just like terms like "immortal" and "post-mortem". But in English, "Mort" is just a name, with variations like "Morty" or "Morton" or "Mortimer". So now you have a general idea going on, and an opportunity for a joke about his parents' foresight when it comes to naming him. Now, what to do about the last name... The basis of this trope is that these characters have an ordinary name based on "Mort" and are associated with death somehow, commonly by either being dead themselves, having the ability to communicate with the dead, being a serial killer whose kills have had plot relevance, or working a mundane death-related job, like a mortician. Nothing fancy like Lord Voldemort or Morticia Addams , though the last name is allowed to be not-so-ordinary if the first name is, and ordinary Mort-based last names are also allowed here. But keep in mind, an ordinary name by itself is not a trope , and deadly situations are bound to happen a few times in stories. There needs to be a direct association between this character and a death-related topic, so guys like Morty Smith don't exactly apply here, even if his adventures can get pretty deathly. For other tropes for specifically M names, see Magnus Means Mage and A Saint Named Mary .
